mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment remains above 6.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real sales of new houses indicate a collapsing residential investment cycle, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profitability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to maintain a positive Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer demand and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or the predictive correlation reverses sign during periods of productivity stagnation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below 11700 billion chained 2017 dollars confirms a deepening economic contraction that reduces aggregate demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 drops below the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with near-zero policy rates create a liquidity trap and erode real returns, forcing investors to discount equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS < 0.5% yields positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 800 thousand units alongside real sales below 360 billion dollars signal a structural breakdown in residential demand that suppresses broader economic activity and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the dual-threshold signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline in walk-forward testing.
